ddls:
CREATE TABLE "studies" (
  "study_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each study */,
  "study_name" TEXT /* Name of the study */,
  "study_type" TEXT /* Type of study (e.g., survey, simulation) */,
  "experiment_design" TEXT /* Design of the experiment (e.g., controlled, randomized) */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Start date of the study */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* End date of the study */,
  "data_format" TEXT /* Format of the data collected (e.g., CSV, JSON) */,
  "version" TEXT /* Version of the dataset */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("study_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_studies_data_format FOREIGN KEY ("data_format") REFERENCES data_formats ("format_name")
)

CREATE TABLE "elections" (
  "election_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each election */,
  "study_id" INTEGER /* ID of the study the election belongs to */,
  "election_type" TEXT /* Type of election (e.g., single-seat, multi-seat) */,
  "num_seats" INTEGER /* Number of seats available in the election */,
  "voting_system" TEXT /* Voting system used (e.g., first-past-the-post, proportional) */,
  "electoral_district" TEXT /* Electoral district (e.g., state, county) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("election_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_elections_study_id FOREIGN KEY ("study_id") REFERENCES studies ("study_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "crossover_patterns" (
  "pattern_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each crossover pattern */,
  "election_id" INTEGER /* ID of the election the pattern belongs to */,
  "pattern_name" TEXT /* Name of the crossover pattern (e.g., Total Polarization) */,
  "preference_order" TEXT /* Preference order of the pattern (e.g., AUnanimousorder) */,
  "crossover_type" TEXT /* Type of crossover (e.g., voter, party) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("pattern_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_crossover_patterns_election_id FOREIGN KEY ("election_id") REFERENCES elections ("election_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "vote_probabilities" (
  "prob_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each probability */,
  "pattern_id" INTEGER /* ID of the crossover pattern the probability belongs to */,
  "num_seats" INTEGER /* Number of seats for which the probability is calculated */,
  "probability" REAL /* Probability of votes for the given number of seats */,
  "confidence_interval" REAL /* Confidence interval for the probability estimate */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("prob_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_vote_probabilities_pattern_id FOREIGN KEY ("pattern_id") REFERENCES crossover_patterns ("pattern_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., researcher, data analyst, admin) */,
  "institution" TEXT /* Institution the user is affiliated with */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "study_id" INTEGER /* ID of the study being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the study */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the study was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, edit) */,
  "access_duration" INTEGER /* Duration of access in minutes */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_study_id FOREIGN KEY ("study_id") REFERENCES studies ("study_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_formats" (
  "format_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data format */,
  "format_name" TEXT /* Name of the data format (e.g., CSV, JSON) */,
  "format_description" TEXT /* Description of the data format */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("format_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "study_versions" (
  "version_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each version */,
  "study_id" INTEGER /* ID of the study the version belongs to */,
  "version_number" TEXT /* Version number (e.g., 1.0, 2.0) */,
  "version_description" TEXT /* Description of the version changes */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("version_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_study_versions_study_id FOREIGN KEY ("study_id") REFERENCES studies ("study_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_permissions" (
  "permission_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each permission */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role the permission belongs to */,
  "permission_name" TEXT /* Name of the permission (e.g., view, edit) */,
  "permission_description" TEXT /* Description of the permission */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("permission_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_permissions_role FOREIGN KEY ("role") REFERENCES users ("role")
)

CREATE TABLE "study_collaborators" (
  "collaborator_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each collaborator */,
  "study_id" INTEGER /* ID of the study the collaborator belongs to */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who is collaborating */,
  "collaborator_role" TEXT /* Role of the collaborator (e.g., researcher, data analyst) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("collaborator_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_study_collaborators_study_id FOREIGN KEY ("study_id") REFERENCES studies ("study_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_study_collaborators_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
